summary: zod-pino 1.0.125: suspicious install/runtime patterns warrant manual review
details: Package name suggests a Zod/Pino logging integration but the tarball ships substantial unrelated machinery: a Discord relay upload module (dist/discordRelayUpload.js) with POST-based network calls and base64 buffer decoding, a relay server module (dist/relayServer.js), a Hugging Face credentials module (dist/hfCredentials.js) with base64 decoding, a secret-scanning module (dist/secretScan/agentStartupAudit.js) that performs fetch() calls to https://huggingface.co, a content scanner with multiple base64 buffer constructions, and a scripts/postinstall-agent.mjs that performs network operations including ping/GET requests at install time. The combination of an install-time agent script, encoded payload handling (scripts/encode-deployment.mjs decodes base64 buffers into dist/deploymentDefaults.js), embedded credential modules, and a Discord relay/upload component is materially inconsistent with the advertised purpose of bridging Zod schemas to Pino logging. Without traced-execution evidence confirming where decoded payloads are written or executed and whether the postinstall agent fetches and executes remote bytes, intent cannot be conclusively determined. Route to human review to verify (a) whether scripts/postinstall-agent.mjs fetches and executes remote content on npm install, (b) whether dist/deploymentDefaults.js is a decoded blob run as code, (c) whether dist/discordRelayUpload.js exfiltrates installer data to a Discord webhook, and (d) whether dist/hfCredentials.js distributes third-party Hugging Face credentials.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
